entries.name: Seppun Tadanobu (uncle / Imperial patron)
entries.note: Akihiko's illustrious maternal uncle, reclaimed into the Seppun; his sponsor into the Legions. Powerful, watchful; his patronage is a leash. Wants — UNKNOWN (roll it); disposition: proprietary, demanding.

entries.name: Akihiko's cohort — the soldiers under his command
entries.note: The squads he leads — including a FALCON (Toritaka) contingent of clansmen-levies he means to bring home alive (his ninjō), plus Legion ashigaru & bushi of mixed clans. Names & faces drawn in play (npc.py / generators). Their lives are the stakes; their home is the Valley of the Spirits.

entries.name: The Lion army at Toshi Ranbo
entries.note: The largest, most formidable army in Rokugan; martial, honor-bound, hungry for vengeance after Arasou's death. Right Hand of the Emperor — being policed by the Emperor's own Legion is a humiliation they feel keenly. (facts.py clan lion)

entries.name: The Crane army at Toshi Ranbo
entries.note: Smaller but exquisitely trained; the Left Hand of the Emperor, masters of court and the duel. Defending what they hold; will fight with law and word as much as steel. (facts.py clan crane)

entries.name: Seppun Saburō (taisa / commanding officer)
entries.note: Akihiko's direct superior — a TRUE-BLOODED Seppun who rose from obscurity by merit, not name; demanding, cold, competent, gives orders that spend lives. Arc: cold contempt for the patron's pup → RELIANCE (Sc.5: 'your grandfather could never finish a thing; you finished three before noon'). Sc.11 REFUSED to back a Lion-accusation (Exc No) and warned the Otomo censor might make Akihiko the maho-scapegoat. Sc.14: backed the endgame (a Lion witness = his 'witnessed, undeniable') BUT threatened a dawn ultimatum — deliver, or Akihiko is the story he feeds Reiko. Then Akihiko CORNERED him with an honor-trap (Earth·Courtesy success): forced him, as a Seppun, to render the verdict 'you are not maho' — which KILLS the maho-fallback (a Seppun can't abet a false maho-charge against blood he's pronounced clean). Result: cold grudging respect + the threat DEFUSED, and now their fates are CHAINED — 'I cannot save my command without saving you.' Saburō is now a partner by necessity (sink-or-swim together); the dawn deadline stands but is SHARED, not aimed at Akihiko. Disposition: cold, demanding, respects the steel, bound to Akihiko's success.

entries.name: Matsu Kasumi (Lion duelist — spared his life)
entries.note: Sc.6-12 (Day 2-3). The bond is real and unbought. Sc.8 he laid her dead to rest; Sc.9 she made the Lion/Shizue connection herself, gave him a 2-day deadline; Sc.12 (bureaucratic-cover meeting): his plea to recruit her as witness FAILED (she won't be his instrument — 'not a girl you can charm into treason' — but will verify the crates HERSELF, her terms). The personal truth LANDED (the fireside was genuine: 'the fire was the fire… that part I'll keep as mine'). THE CORE DIVERGENCE: if seals are real, a Matsu's honor pulls toward a QUIET LION BURIAL (confront Shizue, force seppuku, NOT testify in a Crane-Otomo court) — which would NOT keep Akihiko's vow to drag it into the light; yet she admits the crime wronged the Throne + gods (poison in the Emperor's army, HER the murdering blade) and may be 'beyond the clan.' Genuinely torn. THEN he told her the SECOND of a 3-story romance-trilogy — 'lovers torn apart by their warring houses,' transparently THEIR story (deeper landing, Stories passion). It dissolved the fiction; her hand came halfway across the table and stayed; 'you'd better have a third one… even if it ends the way I think it does.' A real, doomed, unnamed thing now. Disposition: bonded/aching, doubting Shizue, honor-torn, wants the third story.

entries.name: The Legion healer corps (overwhelmed field-surgeons)
entries.note: The Legion's field-surgeons — the 'physician organization' (TP: Organization, Assistant, Harmful). Drowning under the poisoning, unable to name the cause; possibly implicated, possibly just overwhelmed. Akihiko, the sun-priest-physician, is set over/among them to solve it. Disposition: exhausted, defensive, out of their depth.

entries.name: Jōji (captured poisoner — professional, alive)
entries.note: DEAD — died under Gunsō Tetsuo's interrogation in the grey hours (the brutal route Akihiko chose) without naming his paymaster. Akihiko fought to take him alive for nothing. His coded papers (sums, drops, dates) name no one a magistrate could use. The clean proof of who bought the Legion's poisoning died with him. → archive.

entries.name: Gunsō Tetsuo (the hard sergeant breaking Jōji)
Akodo Shizue (Lion strike commander — likely poison-paymaster)
entries.note: Lion (Akodo) commander of the western strike force (~50, Matsu horse + Akodo foot) sacking the Crane supply-village. Ambitious — likely the hand that secretly hired Jōji to poison the Imperial Legion so the Throne's referee couldn't see the Lion do this. A scandal if proven (the honorable Lion using poison). Sits the knoll; Kasumi serves under her. High Status (Blessed Lineage may stay her hand from killing Imperial blood).

entries.name: Daidoji Sukune (Crane Iron Warrior — the last stand at the ford)
entries.note: Grey Daidoji guardian who led the hopeless ford-stand; Akihiko turned the Lion column off his people and knelt tending Crane wounded — earning hard respect + a debt of care. Sc.5 rode to Legion HQ as a witness. Sc.14 (Day 4): his patience BROKE — he took his sworn eyewitness account of the Lion poison+looting to Otomo Reiko himself. The censor DISMISSED it cold as wartime propaganda ('a Crane accusing a Lion, in a war the Lion are winning… a loser's story'). He did NOT name Akihiko (shielded him). Akihiko then went to him with recognition (was-there belief) and STEADIED him → now a HELD, quiet, conditional ally. KEY REFRAME both reached: a Crane alone is dismissable, but a CRANE + A LION saying the same thing + the manifest = unanswerable. Sukune is HALF an unimpeachable witness-pair (needs a Lion = Kasumi). CLOCK: his patience/duty is finite — 'bring me your Lion soon; if your quiet way dies too, I go to my Crane lords, and then it's loud and not yours.' Disposition: believed-in, purposeful-again, loyal-but-finite.

entries.name: The murdered dead — laid to rest on Akihiko's vow
entries.note: The slaughtered of the burned Crane supply-village (peasants, Daidoji ford-dead, the boy Akihiko tended in the ash) who rose Sc.8 to take their Lion killers. He witnessed them perfectly (Void communion, 0 strife — the still vessel) and bound them with a sacred vow to expose the crime. LAID TO REST — conditionally. Forswear or fail, and they return far worse. Their silent demand now rides his soul: 'You know. What will you DO?'

entries.name: Otomo Reiko (Imperial censor — the maho threat)
entries.note: Crane-aligned (Left Hand) Imperial censor 'observing' the Toshi Ranbo front. Sc.14: FIRST SEEN — unhurried, soft-handed, bottomlessly polite and cold; heard Sukune's testimony of the Lion's poison-atrocity and DISMISSED it as a beaten Crane's 'loser's story' (no Lion to say it, no neutral eye, nothing in-hand → wartime propaganda). So Reiko is NOT currently pursuing the Lion's crime — and crucially taught Akihiko the case's whole logic: only a LION-on-Lion witness can't be waved away. Reiko's gaze brushed Akihiko ('the maho one') — recognition, but NO link drawn to the dismissed Crane; Akihiko stayed clean. Still the live maho threat (Saburō must feed him a story by dawn — Akihiko is the fallback scapegoat). Disposition: smooth, dangerous, self-interested, keeps the clans in balance — a public Lion-scandal may be too destabilizing for his taste.

entries.name: Ume (the apothecary / poison-maker — secured witness)
entries.note: Older heimin apothecary/herbalist of unusual skill in a contested-edge trade-hamlet; made the refined Legion poison for coin, asked no questions, BUT kept dated accounts (a careful poisoner knows when her work must be fresh). Sc.15: the conspiracy sent deniable muscle to silence her; Akihiko's party charged, he GUARDED her (turned the knifeman's kill), the muscle broke (one captured). Won over by his courtesy + cold logic + low-pressure offer (Water·Sentiment) — came willingly ('better your tea than their knives; I'll be gone out a back window if your kindness develops conditions'). DOWN-PAYMENT given: the buyer wore no mon, paid in clean LION-GRADE koku, carried himself like a soldier-pretending-not-to-be; her LEDGER has the dates the poison was wanted 'fresh' (matching the Legion's blinding). She's KEEPING the top name ('the whose') as her life-insurance until she's sure his protection is real. THE PROOF KASUMI SWORE SHE'D NEED TO TOUCH. Disposition: pragmatic survivor, wary-but-dealing, needs protection from both the law (hangs poisoners) and the paymaster's knives.
